events.title: US Strikes Indian-Crewed Tankers Off Oman
events.desc: The US military conducted strikes on commercial vessels off the coast of Oman, leading to the deaths of three Indian sailors on the Palau-flagged oil tanker Settebello. India summoned the US deputy chief of mission to protest the action, marking the second such summoning within three days. This incident has heightened tensions between India and the US.

events.title: Israel Strikes Southern Lebanon, Killing 17
events.desc: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon resulted in the deaths of 17 individuals, with nine fatalities reported in the town of Tayr Debba alone. This escalation follows reports of Iran warning Israel to cease attacks on its Lebanese ally, Hezbollah. The strikes occurred despite an ongoing, albeit fragile, ceasefire.

events.title: Iran and US Exchange Strikes Across Middle East
events.desc: Iran claims to have responded to US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain. Bahrain reported damage to its capital overnight following these exchanges. This marks the second consecutive day of cross-regional strikes, significantly increasing regional tensions.

events.title: Israel Strikes Beirut Suburb Following Hezbollah Fire
events.desc: Israel launched attacks on a Beirut suburb, stating the action was in response to Hezbollah firing missiles into Israeli territory. This strike comes shortly after a US-brokered truce, raising concerns about the stability of the ceasefire and the potential for further escalation between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: US Strikes Iran in Retaliation for Helicopter Downing
events.desc: The United States conducted strikes against Iran in response to the downing of a US military helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz. President Donald Trump had previously accused Iran of the attack and vowed retaliation. The incident represents a direct kinetic engagement between the two nations.

events.title: One Injured in Shooting Incident in Israel
events.desc: A shooting incident in Israel resulted in one fatality and five injuries. The suspect reportedly fired from a vehicle at multiple locations before being neutralized by security forces. The motive and full context of the attack are still under investigation.

events.title: Funeral Held for Baby Killed by Israeli Troops
events.desc: A funeral was held for a seven-month-old infant who was fatally shot by Israeli troops in the occupied West Bank. The infant's body was carried by his father, wrapped in a Palestinian flag, to the burial site. This incident highlights ongoing violence and casualties in the region.

events.title: US Army Helicopter Crew Rescued Near Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: A sea drone, resembling a speedboat, successfully rescued two US Army soldiers whose Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz. The incident involving the helicopter's crash is under investigation.

events.title: Israel and Iran Pause Strikes, Warn of Retaliation
events.desc: Both Israel and Iran have announced a pause in their strikes, with Israel's Prime Minister stating the country is holding fire 'at the moment.' Iran's armed forces confirmed they have ceased military action. However, both sides have issued warnings of retaliation should the ceasefire be breached again.

events.title: Air Raids and Artillery Strikes in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: One person was injured following air raids and artillery strikes by Israel on southern Lebanon. This event occurs amidst reports of a potential draft peace deal between Iran and the US that could include ending the conflict in Lebanon.

events.title: Iran Insists on Nuclear Enrichment in Any US Deal
events.desc: Iran has stated its insistence on the right to enrich uranium and maintain control over the Strait of Hormuz as part of any potential deal with the United States. This stance contrasts with Israeli expectations that Iran would be stripped of enriched nuclear material, indicating significant divergence in negotiations.

events.title: US Deportation Flight Carrying Iranians En Route to Central African Republic
events.desc: A US deportation flight reportedly carrying nationals from Iran, Afghanistan, Turkey, and Georgia is en route to the Central African Republic (CAR). This deportation aligns with a crackdown on immigration, despite US State Department advisories warning against travel to CAR due to instability.
